lede: A look at July 31 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Louis de Funès and Stephanie Kwolek, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Fidel Castro
events.desc: Fidel Castro hands over power to his brother, Raúl.

events.title: Georgia (country)
events.desc: The nation of Georgia joins the United Nations.

events.title: The Troubles
events.desc: The Troubles: In Operation Motorman, the British Army re-takes the urban no-go areas of Northern Ireland. It is the biggest British military operation since the Suez Crisis of 1956, and the biggest in Ireland since the Irish War of Independence. Later that day, nine civilians are killed by car bombs in the village of Claudy.

events.title: Loss of MV Darlwyne
events.desc: The pleasure cruiser MV Darlwyne disappeared off the Cornwall coast with the loss of all 31 aboard.

events.title: The Holocaust
events.desc: The Holocaust: Under instructions from Adolf Hitler, Nazi official Hermann Göring orders SS General Reinhard Heydrich to "submit to me as soon as possible a general plan of the administrative material and financial measures necessary for carrying out the desired Final Solution of the Jewish question."

events.title: Bulgaria
events.desc: Bulgaria signs a non-aggression pact with Greece and other states of Balkan Antanti (Turkey, Romania, Yugoslavia).

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The Battle of Passchendaele begins near Ypres in West Flanders, Belgium.

events.title: Patrick Francis Healy
events.desc: Patrick Francis Healy became the first African-American inaugurated as president of a predominantly white university, Georgetown University.

events.title: Second Continental Congress
events.desc: The U.S. Second Continental Congress passes a resolution that the services of Gilbert du Motier, Marquis de Lafayette "be accepted, and that, in consideration of his zeal, illustrious family and connexions, he have the rank and commission of major-general of the United States."

events.title: Pope Sergius IV
events.desc: Pope Sergius IV becomes the 142nd pope, succeeding Pope John XVIII.

births.name: Stephanie Kwolek
births.line: American chemist and engineer, invented Kevlar

births.name: Deval Patrick
births.line: American lawyer and politician, 71st Governor of Massachusetts

deaths.text: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry, French pilot and poet
Andrew Johnson, American general and politician, 17th President of the United States

observances.text: Earliest day on which the Feast of Kamál (Perfection) can fall, while August 1 is the latest; observed on the first day of the eighth month of the Baháʼí calendar. (Baháʼí Faith)
End of the Trinity term (sitting of the High Court of Justice of England)
Lā Hae Hawaiʻi Day (Hawaii, United States), and its related observance: Sovereignty Restoration Day (Hawaiian sovereignty movement)
